classification.stance_proposed: research_blocked; do not advance to a security decision until the critical acceptance tests are met.

deep_dive.executive_summary: At $131.89 (Yahoo close 2026-07-23), our base case annual return is -2.94% per year over seven years on proof-backed component economic value $107 per share. Citigroup is a global G-SIB in mid-cycle credit conditions with standardized CET1 13.2% versus 11.6% required. FY2025 capital return to common shareholders was $17.6 billion. The four-component proof schedule sums to $107/sh base (tangible equity $97 + franchise $15 + excess capital $10 − stress reserve $15). Moat is narrow on deposit and transaction banking scale; dhando is partial at tangible book with bounded downside reserve. Propo...

human_review.model_stance: research_blocked; do not advance to a security decision until the critical acceptance tests are met.

investment_committee.strongest_dissent: The discount to accounting capital is justified because sub-cost-of-equity returns, transformation spending and regulatory constraints can persist while stress losses consume apparent excess capital.
unresolved_items: Segment tangible capital and through-cycle returns cannot be independently reproduced.

decision.primary_power_zone: Credit, funding, and normalized financial returns
decision.next_action: Close critical evidence gaps before freezing a decision-grade packet.

options.falsifier: Primary evidence or through-cycle results support economics below the low case or reveal an omitted claim.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.strongest_dissent: The discount to accounting capital is justified because sub-cost-of-equity returns, transformation spending and regulatory constraints can persist while stress losses consume apparent excess capital.
unresolved_items: Segment tangible capital and through-cycle returns cannot be independently reproduced.
committee.next_action: Close the committee's primary-evidence blockers before requesting an owner decision.

gaps.question: Reconcile regulatory minimums, management buffers, stress losses, transformation costs, and the timing of actual capital return.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary filings or contracts plus an independently reproducible calculation and relevant cross-check.
gaps.acceptance_test: The evidence reproduces the low/base/high bridge, resolves overlap, and identifies a falsifier and monitoring source.
gaps.progress_note: met 2026-07-23: CET1 headroom walk in excess-capital proof; timing and SCB remain judgment.

gaps.question: Reconcile segment tangible capital, through-cycle credit costs, expenses, taxes, and normalized RoTCE.
gaps.evidence_required: Primary filings or contracts plus an independently reproducible calculation and relevant cross-check.
gaps.acceptance_test: The evidence reproduces the low/base/high bridge, resolves overlap, and identifies a falsifier and monitoring source.
gaps.progress_note: met 2026-07-23: firmwide excess-return proof attached; segment capital allocation still undisclosed in filings.

gaps.question: Segment tangible capital and through-cycle returns cannot be independently reproduced.
gaps.evidence_required: Resolve the committee's missing fact with primary evidence and update the affected valuation proof row.
gaps.acceptance_test: The committee item is explicitly closed, sourced, and reflected in the valuation range or documented as immaterial.

method_fit.label: Credit, funding, and normalized financial returns
applicability_tests: The balance sheet and funding structure are the operating engine rather than a financing afterthought.
Credit losses, reserve adequacy, and normalized returns can be measured through a cycle.
Regulatory capital and distributable excess capital can be reconciled.
Asset marks, liabilities, off-balance-sheet claims, and dilution are included once.
failure_modes: industrial EV multiple applied to a financial balance sheet

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.cross_check: Range must be reconciled to primary filings, normalized economics, and relevant transaction or public-market evidence before IC approval.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Citigroup 2025 Annual Report: 1.7475bn common shares, $169.618bn tangible common equity, $97.06 tangible book value per share, 7.7% RoTCE, 13.18% CET1.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of C, including every identified operating claim, asset, liability, and option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Citigroup 2025 Annual Report: 1.7475bn common shares, $169.618bn tangible common equity, $97.06 tangible book value per share, 7.7% RoTCE, 13.18% CET1.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are included as separate components or within the stated equity basis.

valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high values incorporate ownership, tax, funding, cycle, and realization adjustments described in the component evidence.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key tangible_common_equity; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence or through-cycle results support economics below the low case or reveal an omitted claim.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Scenario range includes delay and failure outcomes.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Starter probability is an explicit analyst judgment pending base-rate and milestone reconciliation.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Remaining capital and financing are deducted in the stated option range.

timeline.label: Basic EPS moved +17.9% versus the prior period (6.0 to 7.1).
Operating Cash Flow moved +243.9% versus the prior period (19,669.0 to 67,632.0).
FY2025 10-K: returned $17.6B to common shareholders; CET1 13.2% vs 11.6% required
FY2025 record revenue $85.2B across five operating businesses; RoTCE 7.7%
Transformation and technology spend ~$3.3B (+14% YoY) as Jane Fraser simplification program continues
Jane Fraser became CEO; ongoing exit of non-core consumer franchises and organizational simplification
Stress Capital Buffer (SCB) framework links capital return to Fed stress-test outcomes
Post-crisis restructuring and TARP-era dilution; multi-decade simplification from universal bank model

industry.structure: Global systemically important banks compete on deposit franchises, trading and banking scale, and regulatory capital efficiency; top-tier US peers include JPMorgan, Bank of America, and Wells Fargo.
industry.share_shift: Citigroup is shrinking non-core consumer footprints while investing in Services, Markets, and US Personal Banking; peers with stronger domestic deposit franchises trade at higher price-to-tangible-book multiples.
industry.trend: Higher rates support net interest income but credit normalization, regulatory capital rules, and technology transformation costs pressure returns on tangible common equity.
